commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a skill-status platform mismatch flag and changes `openclaw doctor` workspace output so OS-incompatible skills are rolled up separately from missing requirements.

PR surface: Source +20, Tests +1. Total +21 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current `buildWorkspaceSkillStatus` marks OS-mismatched skills ineligible with `missing.os`, and current `noteWorkspaceStatus` counts them in Missing requirements.
commands.review_followup: - The remaining blockers are concrete and mechanically repairable in the skill status and doctor readiness paths.

target.title: fix(doctor): separate platform-incompatible skills from missing requirements
target.body:
Fixes #89232

## Summary

`openclaw doctor` reported skills with a declared OS requirement that doesn't match the current platform (e.g. macOS-only `apple-notes`, `imsg`, `homebrew` on Linux/Windows) under the same "Missing requirements" count as genuinely-broken installs. Those skills aren't broken — they're inapplicable by design — and the noise hides real issues.

This adds a `platformIncompatible` flag to the skill status entry (true when a skill declares an OS requirement that excludes `process.platform`) and updates the doctor workspace status so platform-mismatched skills are excluded from the "Missing requirements" count and surfaced as a single `Incompatible (platform mismatch, auto-skipped): N` line — matching the issue's "at most one line" expectation.

- `src/skills/discovery/status.ts` — compute and expose `platformIncompatible` on each skill status entry
- `src/commands/doctor-workspace-status.ts` — drop platform-incompatible skills from "Missing requirements" and add the single rollup line

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** On a host whose platform doesn't match a skill's declared OS requirement, the skill is no longer counted as a "missing requirement" in `openclaw doctor`; it is reported once under an "Incompatible (platform mismatch)" rollup, so genuinely-broken installs stay visible.

**Real environment tested:** Linux x86_64, Node.js v22, HEAD `ab5b84a335baba24b081a2d380376b22ca796047` based on `origin/main` `086274fd7e7a33d7085c95373b19cd18945c4b59`. Production function `buildWorkspaceSkillStatus` driven directly via `node --import tsx`, plus the existing/updated Vitest suites.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```
# Drive the production status builder with a macOS-only skill on Linux:
node --import tsx -e 'Promise.all([import("./src/skills/discovery/status.ts"), import("./src/skills/test-support/test-helpers.ts")]).then(([m,h])=>{
  const mk=(n,meta)=>({skill:h.createCanonicalFixtureSkill({name:n,description:"t",filePath:"/tmp/"+n,baseDir:"/tmp",source:"test"}),frontmatter:{},metadata:meta});
  const r=m.buildWorkspaceSkillStatus("/tmp/ws",{entries:[mk("mac-only",{os:["darwin"],requires:{}}),mk("portable",{})]});
  for(const s of r.skills) console.log(s.skillKey,"platformIncompatible="+s.platformIncompatible,"eligible="+s.eligible);
  const old=r.skills.filter(s=>!s.eligible&&!s.disabled&&!s.blockedByAllowlist).length;
  const neu=r.skills.filter(s=>!s.eligible&&!s.disabled&&!s.blockedByAllowlist&&!s.platformIncompatible).length;
  const inc=r.skills.filter(s=>s.platformIncompatible&&!s.disabled&&!s.blockedByAllowlist).length;
  console.log("BEFORE Missing="+old,"AFTER Missing="+neu,"Incompatible="+inc);
})'

# Regression suites:
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
  src/skills/discovery/status.test.ts \
  src/skills/discovery/status-workspace.test.ts \
  src/commands/doctor-workspace-status.test.ts --reporter=dot
```

**Evidence after fix:**
```
platform: linux
  mac-only  platformIncompatible=true  eligible=false
  portable  platformIncompatible=false eligible=true
BEFORE Missing=1 AFTER Missing=0 Incompatible=1

status.test.ts:                 Tests 12 passed (12)
status-workspace + doctor:      Tests  6 passed (6)
```

**Observed result after fix:** The macOS-only skill on Linux resolves to `platformIncompatible=true` and drops out of the doctor "Missing requirements" count (1 → 0), appearing instead under the single "Incompatible (platform mismatch)" rollup. A portable skill stays `platformIncompatible=false`. `tsgo:core` passes.

**What was not tested:** A live full `openclaw doctor` run on Windows with the real ClawHub bundle (the reporter's environment); the platform-mismatch branch is exercised on Linux against macOS-only skills via the production status builder and unit suites, which is the same code path.
target.author: alix-007

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
